title: Why Tilray's Marijuana Rescheduling Sizzle Has Fizzled
text: On April 22, 2026, Tilray stock experienced a dramatic intraday swing following news that the Department of Justice planned to reschedule marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III. The stock surged from approximately $7 to $8 per share within hours, only to retreat to around $6 by day's end—a pattern mirrored across the cannabis sector. The initial rally proved short-lived for several key reasons: The DOJ announcement only granted rescheduling for FDA-approved and state-regulated medical marijuana products. Full rescheduling remains pending an expedited administrative hearing process, making immediate relief uncertain. While rescheduling matters significantly—moving cannabis out of heroin's legal category and eliminating certain tax penalties—it addresses only part of the puzzle. Even complete rescheduling wouldn't matter much for cannabis companies like Tilray without federal legalization. Tilray faces persistent company-specific challenges: ongoing losses and disappointing performance from its cannabis-infused beverage line. The publication recommends investors stick to the sidelines until these issues and broader regulatory progress improve.
